name: MixtarRVS Base Closure 0006
purpose: Inventory the live service supervisor, networking, SSH, device, D-Bus, package, and compatibility dependencies that still come from the Alpine/OpenRC bootstrap layer, then define the MixtarRVS components required before Alpine can stop being the system identity.

current_runtime_roles.device_mounts: OpenRC sysinit services devfs, procfs, sysfs, mdev, hwdrivers
current_runtime_roles.low_level_compat: BusyBox applets and POSIX compatibility paths

mixtar_required_replacements.minimum: Mixtar service runner with list/start/stop/status, ordered startup, shutdown hooks, log capture, and explicit fallback to OpenRC while incomplete.
Mixtar initramfs/runtime contract must own /dev, /proc, /sys, /run, /tmp and expose /System/Devices, /System/Process, /System/Hardware, /System/Runtime/run, /Temporary.
Mixtar network profile reader, interface discovery, DHCP/static config handoff, route/resolver audit, and health check. dhcpcd can stay as backend until replaced.
Mixtar Wi-Fi profile/state layer over iwd first, with backend boundary explicit. iwd can stay as backend until replaced.
Mixtar remote access policy and service manifest for sshd or a future Mixtar agent. OpenSSH can stay as backend until replaced.
Mixtar builder/generation manager owns installed system state. apk can stay as a compatibility backend but must not be the user-facing identity.
Mixtar declares whether dbus is base, workstation-only, or backend dependency for iwd/desktop. Until then dbus remains bootstrap runtime.

non_goals: Do not stop, restart, enable, or disable services.
Do not remove OpenRC, BusyBox, apk, dhcpcd, iwd, dbus, or OpenSSH.
Do not hide POSIX compatibility paths yet.
known_limitations: Stage 0006 is an inventory and closure plan, not a service replacement.
OpenRC remains the live service supervisor.
dhcpcd and iwd remain live networking backends.
OpenSSH remains the live remote access path.
apk remains installed as bootstrap package tooling.
